titlestmt.titleproper: Pasadena Jewish Temple and Center records
Pasadena Jewish Temple and Center records LSC.2383
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Jasmine Larkin, 2020.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated by Caroline Cubé, 2026 March 5.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-05 14:26:52 -0800.

did.unittitle: Pasadena Jewish Temple and Center records

physdesc.extent: (1 unprocessed hard drive, 451 unprocessed optical discs, 57 unprocessed floppy disks)

did.abstract: The Pasadena Jewish Temple and Center (PJTC) records document the history of the Jewish communities in the western San Gabriel Valley. The PJTC is an intergenerational congregation based in Pasadena, California. The collection includes, but is not limited to, PJTC newsletters, board minutes, ephemera, scripts of original musicals, yearbooks, membership directories, financial reports, booklets, photographs, video, and audio recordings. Materials date from 1932 to 2019.
did.physloc: Portions of the collection stored off-site. Advance notice is required for access to the collection. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Property rights to the objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: CONTAINS UNPROCESSED AUDIO, AUDIOVISUAL, AND DIGITAL MATERIALS: Materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Reproduction and Use
userestrict.p: Copyright to portions of this collection has been assigned to the UCLA Library Special Collections. The library can grant permission to publish for materials to which it holds the copyright. All requests for permission to publish must be submitted in writing to Library Special Collections. Credit shall be given as follows: The Regents of the University of California on behalf of the UCLA Library Special Collections.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the Pasadena Jewish Temple and Center, 2019.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Pasadena Jewish Temple and Center records (Collection 2383). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Box level inventory created by Alex Adame, under the supervision of Jasmine Larkin, 2020. The materials are arranged in the existing order in which they were received from the donor. Final processing by Kelly Besser and Laila Jaffer, 2025.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: Administrative History
bioghist.p: The Pasadena Jewish Temple and Center (PJTC) is an intergenerational congregation based in Pasadena, California. The history of the PJTC starts with the Temple B'nai Israel, established in 1921 in Pasadena, and its temple opened in 1923. In 1949, B'nai Israel was renamed the Pasadena Jewish Community under Rabbi Max Vorspan. In 1956, under the leadership of Rabbi Maurice Galpert, the temple became the Pasadena Jewish Temple and Center.
Source: Rutland, Zachary, The Pasadena Jewish Temple and Center Archive report (Pasadena Jewish Temple and Center records collection file, October 2014).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Pasadena Jewish Temple and Center (PJTC) records document the history of the Jewish communities in the western San Gabriel Valley. The collection includes, but is not limited to, PJTC newsletters, board minutes, ephemera, scripts of original musicals, yearbooks, membership directories, financial reports, booklets, and photographs. In addition, the collection includes digitized documents, photographs, video, and audio recordings of events and interviews. Materials date from 1932 to 2019.

did.unittitle: Newsletters, source material, Flame documents
History Committee, Minutes Source Material, 2000 Long Range Plan, Synagogue 2000
Committees, minutes, insurance policies, Sisterhood, Man of the Year Awards, High Holiday Booklet, Vice President religious affairs material
Source material, member data, financial statements and reports, discourse on Jewish practices and specific holy texts
Discourse on Jewish practices and specific holy texts, newspaper clippings
Discourse on Jewish practices and holy texts, newspaper clippings
Discourse on Jewish practices and holy texts, newspaper clippings
Discourse on Jewish practices and holy texts, newspaper clippings
Discourse on Jewish practices and holy texts, newspaper clippings
Discourse on Jewish practices and holy texts, newspaper clippings
High Holy Day sermons, assorted sermons and practices
Assorted sermons, newspaper clippings, Jewish practices discourse
Assorted PJTC correspondences and documents
Lecture notes, introductions to Jewish practices and beliefs, courses

phystech.p: BOX CONTAINS UNPROCESSED AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.
Folder 32 contains one DVD entitled Mikveh Memories.
